Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
David L. Call papers
Call, David L., 1932-
21-2-1620
68.6 cubic feet. 68.6 cubic feet.
1977-1995. Minutes, reports, statements, correspondence, speeches and other items. Records of Dean David L. Call include materials pertaining to various departments of the college; other colleges at Cornell University; the Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, New York; the Cornell Board of Trustees and administration; Cornell Plantations; SUNY; CALS Alumni Association; Isles of Shoals; the Sea Grant Institute; Boyce Thompson Institute; New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets; New York State Council of Farmer Cooperatives; the Council of Agricultural Organizations; Agway, Inc., Agricultural Resources Commission; and the Environmental Protection Agency. General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M. Sampson
Date completed:
August 1996
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, April 2003
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, July 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Dean, New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (CALS), 1977-1995.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Records of Dean David L. Call include materials pertaining to various departments of the college; other colleges at Cornell University; the Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, New York; the Cornell Board of Trustees and administration; Cornell Plantations; SUNY; CALS Alumni Association; Isles of Shoals; the Sea Grant Institute; Boyce Thompson Institute; New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets; New York State Council of Farmer Cooperatives; the Council of Agricultural Organizations; Agway, Inc., Agricultural Resources Commission; and the Environmental Protection Agency. Included are minutes, reports, statements, correspondence, speeches and other items concerning pesticide use and environmental issues, bovine hormone use, agricultural extension, rural development, pest control, migrant labor, agricultural policies, soil and water conservation, legislative bills, investments, land-grant affairs, academic funding and tuition, development, budgets, admissions, faculty salaries and tenure, staff relations, affirmative action and minority affairs, sex discrimination, education programs, libraries, computers, campus planning and development, university policies and procedures, and other matters. Major correspondents include Frank Rhodes, Henry Uihlein, and Roy Carver. Also photographs and items relating to his retirement. Also materials relating to the Unimal and a Chinese vase presented to Call by President Lee Teng-hui of Taiwan.
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Correspondence with George A. West regarding Theta Alpha fraternity, Cornell Beta Chapter, including a list of early members added to the Theta Alpha collection, #37-4-2196. Biographical information, articles, and a photograph added to faculty biographical file, #47-10-3394.
Cite As:
David L. Call papers, #21-2-1620. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 1-67 restricted to the permission of the Dean's Office, New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences.
